messages.content:
Can I have a different VBA code that does waht this code is supposed to do.
Unforntunately the code does not update values to column 11 when I enter a value in column1.

Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
If Not Application.CalculationState = xlDone Then Exit Sub
If Target.Column = 1 Then
Application.EnableEvents = False
Application.ScreenUpdating = False
UpdateColumn11
Application.EnableEvents = True
Application.ScreenUpdating = True

End If
    TimeStampSimple Target, "G2", "J", "mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss", False
End Sub

Sub UpdateColumn11()
Dim lastRow As Long
With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
' Find the last row with data
lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row

' Fill in column 11 with the VLOOKUP results
.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"

End With
End Sub

Here is an alternative VBA code that updates values in column 11 when a value is entered in column 1:

Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Column = 1 Then 'check if changed cell is in column 1
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        Dim lastRow As Long
        With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
            'find last row with data
            lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
            'fill in column 11 with vlookup results
            .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"
        End With
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
End Sub

This code checks if the changed cell is in column 1 and updates column 11 accordingly. It eliminates the need for a separate sub for updating column 11. Note that the TimeStampSimple sub has been removed from this code.

With this VBA code, Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
If Target.Column = 1 Then 'check if changed cell is in column 1
Application.EnableEvents = False
Dim lastRow As Long
With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
' find last row with data
lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
' fill in column 11 with vlookup results
.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"
End With
Application.EnableEvents = True
End If
    TimeStampSimple Target, "G2", "J", "mm/dd/yyyy hh:mm:ss", False
End Sub . . . . I am getting an undisclosed error on this line......  .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"

The error might be caused by the reference to a non-existent sheet "LIST" in the VLOOKUP formula. You may need to update the sheet name to match the actual sheet name that contains the lookup table. 

For example, if your lookup table is in Sheet2, you can modify the formula to: 

.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],Sheet2!R2C1:R54C3,3,FALSE),"""")"

Also, make sure that the reference to column 10 (-10) in the formula is correct. If the lookup value is in column 1 and you want to return a value from column 3 of the lookup table, the column offset should be -2, not -10.
The values derived from the code are to be sent into column 11. The lookup value is in column 1. The sheet LIST exists and the values in this sheet are taken from column 3 cell C2 to Cell C54

In that case, you can modify the VLOOKUP formula to refer to Column C (column 3) of the lookup table in the LIST sheet, like this: 

```
.Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!C2:C54,3,FALSE),"""")"
```

This formula assumes that the lookup value is in column A (column 1) and the values to be returned are in column C (column 3) of the LIST sheet. 

Make sure to double-check the sheet name and cell references to avoid potential errors.

I have put the revised line given (   .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!C2:C54,3,FALSE),"""")"   ) but it still does not enter values into column 11 when I change the value in column 1

If the code is not entering values into column 11, there might be other factors that could be causing the issue. Here are some things you can check:

1. Make sure that macros are enabled and that the code is saved in the correct sheet module (not a standard module).

2. Double-check that the worksheet name and column references are correct in your VLOOKUP formula.

3. Try using the below code which avoids the use of formula and paste the values directly using 'Value' property of the range:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        Dim lastRow As Long
        With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
            lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
            'update values in column 11 based on values in column 1
            Dim i As Long
            For i = 2 To lastRow
                If .Cells(i, 1) <> "" Then 'check if cell is not blank
                    'get the corresponding value from the lookup table
                    Dim lookupVal As Variant
                    lookupVal = Application.VLookup(.Cells(i, 1), Worksheets("LIST").Range("A:C"), 3, False)
                    If Not IsError(lookupVal) Then 'check if vlookup was successful
                        .Cells(i, 11).Value = lookupVal 'update value in column 11
                    Else
                        .Cells(i, 11).Value = "" 'clear value in column 11 if vlookup fails
                    End If
                End If
            Next i
        End With
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
End Sub
```

This code uses a loop to go through each row and update the value in column 11 based on the corresponding value in the lookup table. 

If the issue persists, you may want to check if there are any other macros or worksheet event handlers that could be interfering with the code.

The code does not work. If I use my original VLOOKUP formula and click on the calculate button in excel, column 11 shows the value expected. My main issue is that the workbook is set to manual calculation.

If the workbook is set to manual calculation, you can try using the following code which calculates the formula for column 11 before pasting the values:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Change(ByVal Target As Range)
    If Target.Column = 1 Then
        Application.EnableEvents = False
        Dim lastRow As Long
        With Worksheets("PREQUEST")
            lastRow = .Cells(.Rows.Count, "A").End(xlUp).Row
            .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).FormulaR1C1 = "=IF(RC[-10]<>"",VLOOKUP(RC[-10],LIST!C2:C54,3,FALSE),"""")"
            .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).Value = .Range("K2:K" & lastRow).Value 'calculate formulas and paste values
        End With
        Application.EnableEvents = True
    End If
End Sub
```

This code uses the same VLOOKUP formula as before, but adds a second line that calculates the formulas and pastes the values using the `Value` property of the range. This should work even if the workbook is set to manual calculation.

If the issue persists, you may want to check if there are any other workbook-level event handlers or add-ins that could be interfering with your code.
The modified code did not work. I have given up on this and reverted to my original VLOOKUP formula. Can I have a code that enables full calculation of the sheet when it is opened

Sure, you can use the following code to force a full recalculation of the workbook when it is opened:

```
Private Sub Workbook_Open()
    Application.Calculation = xlCalculationAutomatic
    Application.CalculateFullRebuild
End Sub
```

This code sets the calculation mode to automatic and triggers a full recalculation of the workbook using the `CalculateFullRebuild` method. This should ensure that all formulas are up to date and that the VLOOKUP formula in column 11 is calculated correctly.

You can add this code to the 'ThisWorkbook' module in the VBA editor. The code will run automatically whenever the workbook is opened.
will this code enable calculation for other sheets in the workbook

Yes, the code `Application.Calculation = xlCalculationAutomatic` will enable automatic calculation for all sheets in the workbook, not just the sheet that is active at the time the code is executed. 

If you only want to enable automatic calculation for a specific sheet, you can modify the `Workbook_Open` event to include a reference to the sheet, like this:

```
Private Sub Workbook_Open()
    Worksheets("Sheet1").EnableCalculation = True
    Worksheets("Sheet1").Calculate
End Sub
```

This code sets the `EnableCalculation` property for Sheet1 to `True`, and then calculates the sheet using the `Calculate` method. You can replace "Sheet1" with the name of your desired sheet.

Note that any formulas or calculations that involve other sheets will still need to be recalculated manually or using VBA code.
In VBA code how will I calculate a specific sheet when it is opened

You can use the Worksheet_Activate event to calculate a specific sheet when it is activated or opened. Here's an example code that calculates Sheet1 when it is activated:

```
Private Sub Worksheet_Activate()
    If ActiveSheet.Name = "Sheet1" Then
        ActiveSheet.Calculate
     'or use Application.CalculateFullRebuild to calculate the entire workbook
    End If
End Sub
```

This code will trigger the Calculate method for Sheet1 whenever it is activated or opened. If you want to recalculate the entire workbook, you can replace `ActiveSheet.Calculate` with `Application.CalculateFullRebuild`. 

Note that this code should be placed in the sheet module (not in a standard module) for the sheet that you want to calculate.
can you please give me a list of all common application events tha should be turned on. for example Application.EnableEvents = True, Application.ScreenUpdating = True

Sure, here are some common application-level events and properties that you may want to use in your VBA code:

- `Application.EnableEvents`: When this property is set to `True`, Excel will fire events in response to user actions or changes in the workbook. If set to `False`, events will not be triggered. It's a good practice to use `Application.EnableEvents = False` at the beginning of code that modifies the workbook, and then set it back to `True` at the end.

- `Application.ScreenUpdating`: This property controls whether Excel updates the screen display during code execution. When set to `False`, the screen display is turned off and the code runs faster. When set to `True`, the screen display is turned on and the user can see the results of the code as it runs.

- `Application.Calculation`: This property specifies the calculation mode for the workbook. Setting it to `xlCalculationAutomatic` will enable automatic calculation of formulas whenever the workbook is modified. Setting it to `xlCalculationManual` will disable automatic calculation and requires formulas to be calculated manually.

- `Application.StatusBar`: This property allows you to display a message on the status bar at the bottom of the Excel window. This can be useful to show progress or status updates during code execution.

- `Application.DisplayAlerts`: This property controls whether Excel displays messages or warnings to the user when certain actions are performed. Setting it to `False` can prevent interruptions during code execution, but use with caution as it can also suppress important warnings.

- `Application.EnableCancelKey`: This property controls whether the user can break code execution by pressing the ESC key. Setting it to `xlErrorHandler` will allow the user to cancel code execution, while setting it to `False` will disable this feature.

These are just a few examples of common application-level events and properties used in VBA code. Other events and properties may be relevant depending on the specific task or situation.
